article_title: Apache patches a double-free in HTTP/2 that crashes workers with two frames and one TCP connection
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

content_preview.title: Apache patches a double-free in HTTP/2 that crashes workers with two frames and one TCP connection
content_preview.summary: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.67 fixes CVE-2026-23918, a double-free in mod_http2 that triggers on early stream reset and may enable remote code execution on Debian-default builds.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The Apache Software Foundation has released Apache HTTP Server 2.4.67 to fix CVE-2026-23918, a double-free in the server's HTTP/2 implementation that the project's security team rates as "important" and that the National Vulnerability Database scores at CVSS 8.8, according to the [Apache HTTP Server vendor advisory](https://httpd.apache.org/security/vulnerabilities_24.html) and the [NVD entry](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-23918). The bug, classified as CWE-415, sits in ...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Comprehensive 788-word News piece with clear technical chain-of-custody (bug location, trigger sequence, disclosure timeline, RCE conditional). Appropriate hedging on RCE practicality.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 4 sources fetched cleanly (200 OK). Read every snapshot from disk. CVE-2026-23918 verified across all 4 sources. CWE-415 and CVSS 8.8 verified verbatim in NVD (cited authority). CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H vector verified in NVD. h2_mplx.c, HEADERS+RST_STREAM trigger sequence, APR mmap allocator, Debian-derived attack path verified verbatim in TheHackerNews (cited authority). Bartlomiej Dmitruk + striga.ai and Stanislaw Strzalkowski + isec.pl researcher attribution verified in vendor advisory and TheHackerNews. SVN revision r1930444, report date 2025-12-10, fix date 2025-12-11, public release date 2026-05-04, CVE-2026-24072 companion-bug report date 2026-01-20 all verified verbatim in vendor advisory. SecurityWeek 'fixes for 11 vulnerabilities, 10 of which affect all previous releases' verified verbatim. All five direct quotes ('Double Free and possible RCE...', 'one TCP connection, two frames...', 'practical exploitation requires an info leak for system() and the scoreboard offsets', 'attack surface is large as mod_http2 ships in default builds...', 'by triggering an early reset...') verified verbatim in their cited sources.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every specific (CVE numbers, CVSS, CWE, version numbers, code path, dates, SVN revision, researcher names) traces to its cited authority. Multi-source corroboration on major facts.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral, technical, appropriately hedged. 'What We Don't Know' section explicitly scopes uncertainty.
editor_notes.originality: First-of-cluster review for CVE-2026-23918 in this batch (PR #1194 covers the same CVE and will be reviewed downstream).
concerns: The vendor advisory domain `httpd.apache.org` is not in `config/source_allowlist.txt`. It is the canonical Apache HTTP Server security advisory page — the most authoritative possible source for an Apache CVE. The snapshot was fetched cleanly (200 OK) and every claim attributed to it was verified verbatim. Recommend adding `httpd.apache.org` to the allowlist in a follow-up batch.
recommendations: Add `httpd.apache.org` to `config/source_allowlist.txt` in the next allowlist batch.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively a strong, multi-sourced vulnerability-disclosure article with careful technical attribution. The single recoverable issue is editorial-process metadata (allowlist gap on an authoritative vendor source); a corrections clarification covers it honestly while the article publishes.
